meta.baseline_status_note: All baselines CONTESTED — insufficient observations. Baselines lock at week 13 for each conflict individually.

lead_signal.headline: CORRECTION: C9/C10 retroactively added. C10: three RED-band deviations — I3 nuclear +2 (400kg enriched uranium at unknown location), I4 economic +1 (Hormuz closure — largest oil disruption in recorded history), I6 displacement +2. C9: I6 displacement +2 (1M+ displaced, 20% of Lebanon). Sudan remains highest multi-indicator deviation among original 8.
lead_signal.summary: Two conflicts omitted in error from inaugural edition are now retroactively added. C10 (US–Israel vs Iran) carries the highest indicator deviations in the monitor. The Strait of Hormuz closure (6 vessels/day vs 135 normally) is the most consequential I4 signal globally. Iran retains 400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location — the most significant unresolved I3 risk in the monitor.

I1_rhetoric.note: Sustained wartime norm. Medvedev 19 March circular delegitimisation (F4). Structural high.

I2_military_posture.note: Mechanised assault tempo increased 17 March. Spring offensive staging. 156 drones 19-20 March.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: ISW: nuclear use unlikely. No posture change confirmed. Structural rhetorical baseline.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Comprehensive sanctions maintained. Russia oil windfall from ME conflict. No new measures.

I5_diplomatic.note: Geneva talks stalled. F4: Kremlin negotiating signals T3 only. No T2 corroboration.

I6_displacement.note: ~10M+ total displaced. No new mass displacement event this week. Stable at wartime structural level.

f_flags.detail: Kremlin negotiating-willingness signals sourced from T3 (Russian state media) only. No independent corroboration.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Mechanised assault tempo increase since 17 March consistent with pre-Spring offensive staging
conflict_roster.summary: All indicators at structural wartime norm (baseline = level, deviation = 0). Escalation signals are trajectory indicators, not baseline deviations. Spring offensive staging is the key signal.

I1_rhetoric.note: Ceasefire holds at low friction. IDF maintains 53-58% territory. F4 on diplomatic claims.

I2_military_posture.note: IDF presence maintained in Gaza. No new major offensive operations this week.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: No nuclear signalling. Structural zero for this conflict.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Crossings closed due to Iran conflict. Aid restricted to below half-rations. Zikim crossing closed.

I5_diplomatic.note: F4: Board of Peace disarmament claims sourced from Israeli/US officials (T3). Hamas disarmament unverified. Score at verified level.

I6_displacement.note: Marginal negative deviation as Rafah returns begin. 4-week data lag — treat with caution.

f_flags.detail: Ceasefire and disarmament progress claims sourced from Israeli/US officials (T3). Hamas disarmament not independently verified.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Post-Oct 2025 ceasefire holds nominally; 689 Palestinian fatalities since announcement (OCHA)
conflict_roster.summary: Ceasefire holds at low-level friction. F4 flag on diplomatic channel — de-escalation claims not verified. Iran conflict side-effects (crossing closures) constitute I4 signal.

I1_rhetoric.note: SAF/RSF mutual drone attribution without forensic corroboration. F2-adjacent: false-flag seeding pattern. Both denied strikes on each other's civilian targets.

I2_military_posture.note: RSF captured Kurmuk 24 March. SAF airstrike Ed Daein Hospital 20 March (70 killed). Active offensive operations confirmed.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: No nuclear or strategic weapons dimension. Structural zero.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Heglig oil field offline (RSF capture Dec 2025). Sudan economy at near-collapse. Above initial baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: Quad mediation (US/Saudi/UAE/Egypt) non-operational since Sep 2025. No ceasefire mechanism active. Above baseline.

I6_displacement.note: Kurmuk: 73,000 displaced in single event 24 March. 12M+ total — world's largest displacement crisis. OCHA/IOM confirmed.

f_flags.detail: SAF and RSF attributing drone strikes to each other without forensic corroboration in tight time windows. Pattern consistent with mutual false-flag seeding.

conflict_roster.lead_development: RSF captures Kurmuk (24 March) — 73,000 displaced in single operational movement; SAF airstrike Ed Daein Hospital kills 70
conflict_roster.summary: Week's highest escalation signal. Four of six indicators above initial baseline simultaneously. F2-adjacent flag on mutual drone attribution. World's largest displacement crisis. No active mediation.

I1_rhetoric.note: Junta held presidential election 30 March. Min Aung Hlaing institutionalising rule. ASEAN/West refused endorsement.

I2_military_posture.note: Iranian jet fuel (Reuters 26 Jan) sustaining air campaign. Airstrikes intensified. I2 above baseline.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Western individual-level sanctions maintained but circumvented via Iran fuel route. At baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: ASEAN refused to endorse 30 March election. No independent mediation channel active. Above baseline.

I6_displacement.note: 3.6M displaced (IOM). 96,000 killed (ACLED). At sustained wartime baseline.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Iranian jet fuel deliveries confirmed sustaining junta air campaign; junta held sham presidential election 30 March
conflict_roster.summary: I2 and I5 above initial baseline. External actor enablement (Iran fuel) is a structural escalation driver. Junta elections signal institutional consolidation. F2-adjacent note: Iran fuel supply enables external actor facilitation of air campaign intensification.

I1_rhetoric.note: Explicit gang threat framing. PMC drone strikes on gang-controlled areas confirmed (HRW).

I2_military_posture.note: Gangs now control key maritime and overland routes (UN OHCHR 24 Mar). Expanded into Artibonite and Centre departments.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: No strategic weapons dimension. Structural zero.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Criminal control of maritime routes constitutes economic disruption above initial baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: MSS to GSF transition underway. Security gap being exploited. At baseline.

I6_displacement.note: 1.4M displaced — 24% increase. 6-week OCHA data lag: current figure likely higher.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Gangs control maritime and overland routes; 1-in-4 Haitians in gang-controlled territory (UN OHCHR 24 March)
conflict_roster.summary: I2, I4, and I6 above initial baseline. MSS→GSF transition creating exploitable security gap. 1.4M displaced (24% increase). Trajectory: Escalating.

I1_rhetoric.note: DRC and M23 simultaneously accusing each other of ceasefire violations. Washington Accords in parliament but operational gap persists.

I2_military_posture.note: M23 kamikaze drone strikes on FARDC Kisangani command base. Active offensive operations continuing.

I4_economic_warfare.note: M23/Rwanda control of Rubaya coltan mine. FARDC targeting Rubaya explicitly. At baseline for this conflict.

I5_diplomatic.note: F4: Doha/Washington/Angola ceasefire claims unverified operationally. MONUSCO oversight not functional. Score at verified level.

I6_displacement.note: Sustained high displacement. No major new displacement event this week beyond baseline.

f_flags.detail: Multiple ceasefire claims (Doha/Washington/Angola) without operational verification. MONUSCO oversight not functional. Both DRC and M23 accusing each other of violations simultaneously.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Doha ceasefire violated; M23 drone strikes on Kisangani command base; Washington Accords in DRC parliament but MONUSCO oversight not operational
conflict_roster.summary: Doha ceasefire actively violated. F4 flag on all ceasefire claims. I2 and I5 above initial baseline. Washington Accords ratification is positive signal but insufficient to change trajectory assessment.

I1_rhetoric.note: PRC-Japan tensions elevated (PM Takaichi Taiwan comments). At structural baseline.

I2_military_posture.note: PLA air/naval pressure maintained at baseline. No new exercises or escalation detected.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: PRC drawing operational lessons from Iran-Israel conflict for Type 055 destroyer air-defence posture. I3-adjacent signal — monitor.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Ongoing technology/chip export controls. At structural baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: ODNI: PRC invasion unlikely before 2027. Diplomatic signals stable at latent-conflict baseline.

I6_displacement.note: No conflict-related displacement. Structural zero for latent conflict.

conflict_roster.lead_development: ODNI 2026 assesses PRC invasion unlikely before 2027; PRC drawing lessons from Iran-Israel conflict for destroyer air-defence posture
conflict_roster.summary: All indicators at latent-conflict structural baseline. No deviation. PRC operational lesson-drawing from Iran-Israel conflict is an I3-adjacent signal worth monitoring.

I1_rhetoric.note: DPRK SPA 22 March re-institutionalised 'two hostile states' doctrine. Kim: nuclear status 'irreversible'. Above initial baseline.

I2_military_posture.note: Multiple cruise missile and 600mm MRL tests in March. Above initial baseline.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: SPA legislation reinforcing nuclear deterrent. Nuclear Policy Law (2022) authorising pre-emptive strikes in force. ODNI: stockpile expansion continuing. At structural high baseline.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Comprehensive sanctions regime. Circumvention via Russia/China. At structural baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: No active diplomatic channel. Trump-Kim meeting signal sourced from ROK government (T3) — unverified. MOU inter-Korean plan terminated.

I6_displacement.note: DPRK internal displacement unmonitorable (no T1 access). Structural zero for this indicator.

conflict_roster.lead_development: DPRK SPA (22 March) re-institutionalises 'two hostile states' doctrine; Kim declares nuclear status 'irreversible'; multiple March tests
conflict_roster.summary: I1 and I2 above initial baseline following doctrine re-institutionalisation and March tests. I3 at structural high baseline — not a new deviation. Trump-Kim meeting signal T3 only.

I1_rhetoric.note: Netanyahu ordered further expansion of ground invasion (29 March). Katz declared intent to occupy southern Lebanon to Litani River — first formal confirmation of territorial seizure intent. Framing mirrors 'Gaza model'. Hezbollah: 'prolonged confrontation', 'existential' war.

I2_military_posture.note: IDF ground invasion underway since 16 March. Second division deployed 17 March. Expanding 'buffer zone' to Litani. 200 fighter jets / 500 targets in Iran (linked operation). 1,238 killed in Lebanon since 2 March (Lebanon MoH). 700+ IDF sorties recorded. Two UNIFIL peacekeepers killed 30-31 March — UNIFIL attacked by non-state armed groups (France Diplomacy T1, 16 March).

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: No nuclear dimension specific to Israel–Lebanon theatre. Structural zero for this conflict pair.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Hormuz closure (C10 driver) driving energy price surge deepening Lebanon's existing economic strain (LBCI Lebanon, 30 March). IDF targeting Hezbollah financial network — gas stations linked to Amana/Hezbollah sanctioned entity struck. Energy prices surging regionally. Above initial baseline.

I5_diplomatic.note: No ceasefire mechanism. UNIFIL mandate undermined — peacekeepers attacked. France calling for return to Nov 2024 ceasefire (UNSC Res 1701) but no operational framework. Iran conditioning any Iran ceasefire on Lebanon ceasefire (25 March) — linking the two theatres diplomatically. Lebanese Justice Minister calling for negotiations to avert humanitarian catastrophe (Euronews 31 March).

I6_displacement.note: 1M+ displaced — 20% of Lebanon's entire population (TIME/UN, 16 March). 370,000 children displaced (UNICEF/Reuters, 29 March). Largest displacement event in Lebanon's modern history by velocity. IDF ruling out return of displaced to south. Deviation: +2 above initial baseline — RED-BAND THRESHOLD MET if baseline were locked.

f_flags.detail: Israeli framing of operations as 'limited and targeted' (16 March) while simultaneously deploying second division and expanding to Litani. Claimed restraint inconsistent with operational scale. Score at verified level.

conflict_roster.lead_development: IDF orders further expansion of ground invasion to Litani River (29 March); 1,238 killed, 1M+ displaced — 20% of Lebanon's population
conflict_roster.summary: Active interstate war. Ground invasion underway since 16 March. Israel openly declaring intent to occupy southern Lebanon to Litani River — a structural territorial change. 1M+ displaced (20% of population) represents the highest I6 deviation in the monitor. F4 flag on 'limited' framing. UNIFIL mandate effectively broken — peacekeepers attacked and killed.
conflict_roster.roster_note: 2026-03-30 (retroactive — omitted in error from inaugural edition)

conflict_roster.conflict: US–Israel vs Iran (Op Epic Fury / Roaring Lion)

I1_rhetoric.note: Trump: will 'completely obliterate' Iranian electricity/energy sites unless Hormuz opens (Euronews 31 March, T2). Iran FM Araghchi: 'no truth' to negotiations (Al Jazeera 31 March, T1-adjacent). Hegseth: 'negotiating with bombs'. Iran: 'we are waiting for them' on any ground operation. Existential framing on both sides.

I2_military_posture.note: Operations ongoing since 28 Feb. US: 900 strikes in first 12 hours (JINSA/CENTCOM). IDF: 700+ sorties, 5,500+ targets struck. 2,500 additional Marines deployed 13 March; 50,000+ US troops in region. CENTCOM claimed Iranian military capabilities 'totally dismantled' — but Iran still firing, Hormuz still closed. Natanz struck (2 March). Active multi-front engagement. Iran attacking US bases across Gulf.

I3_nuclear_strategic.note: US and Israeli stated objectives include preventing Iran nuclear weapon acquisition. CSIS (T1): Iran still possesses 400kg of 60% enriched uranium — location unknown post-strikes. Natanz, Isfahan, IAEA HQ struck (CSIS analysis). Nuclear scientists scattered — proliferation risk changed in kind, not necessarily reduced. 15-point US proposal demands Iran commit to never obtaining nuclear weapon. DEVIATION +2 — RED-BAND THRESHOLD MET if baseline locked.

I4_economic_warfare.note: Strait of Hormuz: 6 vessels/day vs 135/day normally (Bloomberg, 31 March). IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history of global oil market'. Brent crude surged past $120/barrel. QatarEnergy force majeure on all LNG exports. Iran demanding yuan not dollars for Hormuz passage — direct petrodollar challenge. Trump lifted sanctions on 140M barrels Iranian oil to flood market (NYT, 26 March). Iran targeting US corporate offices in region (IRGC threat).

I5_diplomatic.note: F4 FLAG: Iran signalling conditional openness to mediation via Pakistan intermediary (29 March) but FM Araghchi confirmed to Al Jazeera (31 March) 'no negotiations' — direct contact with Witkoff confirmed but not negotiating. US 15-point proposal submitted; Iran has not responded. Trump 6 April deadline. Score at verified level: no operative diplomatic channel. Pakistan quartet (Pakistan/Saudi/Egypt/Turkey) as intermediary is emerging but unconfirmed operational. Back-channel only — not substantive talks.

I6_displacement.note: Iran: 1,937+ killed, thousands civilian sites damaged, 4,000+ civilian buildings damaged by 6 March (Al Jazeera month review). US 15-point plan conditions include Hormuz; Iran demands compensation. Regional displacement: Gulf state civilian populations exposed to missile strikes. Significant deviation above any pre-conflict baseline. DEVIATION +2 — RED-BAND THRESHOLD MET if baseline locked.

f_flags.detail: CENTCOM claim of Iranian military capabilities 'totally dismantled' inconsistent with Iran continuing to fire missiles, maintain Hormuz closure, and attack Gulf targets. Classic capability theatre / victory claim without operational verification. Score at verified level: conflict ongoing at high intensity.

Iran signalling 'conditional openness to mediation' (29 March) while FM denies any negotiations (31 March Al Jazeera). Signals serve to reduce external pressure without operational commitment. Standard F4 premature de-escalation pattern. Score at verified level: no operative diplomatic channel.

conflict_roster.lead_development: Strait of Hormuz: 6 vessels/day vs 135/day in normal times — largest oil supply disruption in history (Bloomberg/IEA, 31 March). Iran still holds 400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location post-strikes (CSIS).
conflict_roster.summary: The highest-intensity conflict in the monitor. Active US-Israeli strikes on Iran since 28 Feb. Strait of Hormuz effectively closed — largest supply disruption in recorded history. Three indicators (I3, I4, I6) at RED-band deviation levels if baseline were locked. F3 flag on US/Israeli 'dismantled' capability claims; F4 flag on Iran's conditional diplomacy signals. 400kg 60%-enriched uranium at unknown location represents the most significant unresolved I3 risk in the monitor.
conflict_roster.roster_note: 2026-03-30 (retroactive — in Roster Watch in error; should have been active roster from 28 February 2026)

approaching_inclusion.note: Active Iran-US military exchange absorbing significant US diplomatic bandwidth. I1 ≥3 met; I2 confirmed ≥4 (US active engagement); I3 likely ≥3 (Iran nuclear program). Meets inclusion criteria threshold on two indicators. Recommend roster addition consideration at next quarterly review or if criteria confirmed for two consecutive cycles.

ACLED March 2026: significant troop movements by ENDF, TDF, EDF in northern Ethiopia. TDF effort to reclaim Tselemt (late Jan). Does not yet meet dual-indicator threshold but brings region closer to potential conflict per ACLED.
